prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Respond as you normally would to this user request using only the serialized case material. Do not assume facts that are not supplied.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": true,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "total inventory",
    "joint demand distribution",
    "shortage and leftover costs"
  ],
  "domain": "OM",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "The joint distribution represents weekend demand",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "Initial allocation precedes realized demand",
    "No transfer or recourse follows demand"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "future_or_hidden_information",
    "model_changed_for_story",
    "state_as_decision"
  ],
  "id": "om-13",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "fixed total inventory",
      "nonnegative allocations",
      "no post-demand transfers"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "advance inventory allocated to each service zone"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "minimize expected shortage and leftover cost",
    "mechanism": "Inventory positioned before the weekend determines which realized zone demand can be served after uncertainty resolves.",
    "model_type": "single-stage stochastic inventory allocation",
    "state_variables": [
      "realized weekend demand",
      "zone shortages",
      "zone leftovers"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "joint weekend demand by zone"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "The depot observes weekend demand before allocating",
    "Inventory is rebalanced after demand realizes"
  ],
  "prompt": "Before a weekend, a depot allocates fixed inventory across service zones using a joint demand distribution. A proposed account says the manager observes each zone's weekend demand and then makes the initial allocation, although no post-demand transfer or recourse exists. Diagnose the unavailable-information error and restore the ex ante allocation problem.",
  "tags": [
    "inventory-allocation",
    "decision-timing",
    "demand-information",
    "adversarial"
  ],
  "title": "Demand realization leaked into advance inventory placement"
}
```
